title: stemaria alcicornis
intro: Stemaria alcicornis, also known as the Cornish moneywort, is a flowering plant species native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It belongs to the balsam family (Imporiaceae) and is recognized by its green, fleshy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Stemaria alcicornis include treating respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds, and it is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal use.
